data.title: Bullish sentiment rises, shows recent AAII survey
data.description:
Bullish sentiment among individual investors rose for the second consecutive week, showed the recent survey conducted by American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

Bulls as per the AAII sentiment survey stood at 45% for the week ending July 2, compared to 35.1% in the prior update. Meanwhile, bearish sentiment as per the survey fell to 33.1% for the week, versus 40.3% the week before.

Investors kept their focus on the ongoing trade negotiations during the week. The UK-US trade deal coming into force on Monday, slashing US export tariffs for the UK's automotive and aerospace sectors, reignited hope in the market. The U.S. and China also finalized a trade truce reached last month, with Beijing agreeing to supply rare earths in exchange for the lifting of U.S. countermeasures.

“Tariff-related fears temporarily pressured the stock, but recent negotiations have reduced long-term risk, restoring confidence in the growth story,” pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis, adding that while the stock has rebounded nicely from the April dip so far into early July, the shares are “still well priced for several years of growth if the US consumer holds up.”

The benchmark S&P500 (SP500) and Nasdaq (COMP:IND) gained over 1% in the last one week, while Dow (DJI) rose over 2%.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, also fell during the week, with the figure standing at 21.9%, compared to 24.7% last week.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: Baidu shows off search with AI revamp, image-to-video model MuseSteamer
data.description:
Baidu (NASDAQ:BIDU) confirmed that it has revamped its Search with New AI features in its biggest overhaul in a decade.

The Chinese internet search giant also confirmed the launch of its image-to-video model MuseSteamer.

Baidu said that at the heart of this revamp is the upgraded Smart Search Box that supports long-form queries of over 1,000 Chinese characters, accepts multimedia inputs including image, audio, video, and file, and integrates directly with AI tools for writing, image generation.

 

 

Baidu noted that if a user is planning a travel itinerary or trying to recall a vague movie plot, the Smart Search Box can interpret the request and deliver precise, helpful results.  For example, users can take a photo of a scenic spot for instant tour commentary, snap a faulty appliance for troubleshooting, or upload a medical report for AI-powered interpretation.

Baidu added that Search's upgraded Baikan feature restructures search result pages, going beyond traditional links to deliver structured answers that integrate formats including text, images, videos, charts, among others.

With services like AI agents to be integrated, Baikan provides tailored, actionable information — such as organizing product recommendations and even enabling direct purchases from the search page.

The AI Assistant feature now offers video calling, multimodal input (text, voice, image, and files), and outputs including notes, images, and videos, according to the company.

 

 

Baidu noted that upgraded Baidu Search also features advanced content creation capabilities, offering a one-stop solution from ideation to video generation to publishing.

Users can create a three-minute AI-generated video from a single sentence, with built-in tools for storyboard editing and custom scene design, the company added.

Baidu also confirmed the launch of MuseSteamer, an image-to-video model enabling video generation featuring synchronized visuals, sound effects, and dialogue. Baidu is integrating MuseSteamer into Baidu Search.

The model can simultaneously generate synchronized visuals, speech, and sound effects in Chinese. It can produce 10-second 1080P cinematic-quality clips from just one image — complete with nuanced facial expressions and camera motion.

The MuseSteamer model family includes Turbo, Lite, Pro, and a full lineup of voiced versions. The Turbo version is currently available for limited-time free public testing on Huixiang, Baidu's AI video creation platform, with the other versions set to roll out starting in August, the company added.

Baidu was among the first group of Chinese companies whose AI model was approved in China following the launch of OpenAI's ChatGPT in late 2022 in the U.S. However, the company's Ernie Bot faces fierce competition in the Chinese market, with tech giants like Alibaba (BABA) and startups such as MiniMax, Baichuan and Zhipu AI also introducing AI models.

data.title: Trican Well Service Ltd. raises dividend by 10% to $0.055
data.description:
• Trican Well Service Ltd. (TSX:TCW:CA) declares $0.055/share quarterly dividend, 10% increase from prior dividend of $0.050.
  • Forward yield 4.82%
  • Payable Sept. 30; for shareholders of record Sept. 12; ex-div Sept. 12.
  • See TCW:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Embraer tallies 30% increase in Q2 aircraft deliveries
data.description:
Embraer (NYSE:ERJ) +2.4% pre-market Thursday after saying it delivered 61 aircraft during Q2, a 30% increase from a year earlier, when 47 jets were delivered, and more than double the volume recorded in Q1 2025.

The deliveries in the quarter through the end of June included 19 commercial jets, in line with the same quarter last year, 38 executive jets, a 41% Y/Y jump, and four defense aircraft.

The Brazilian planemaker said it expects to deliver 77-85 commercial planes and 145-155 executive jets this year.

Embraer (NYSE:ERJ) shares hit a fresh all-time high in Sao Paulo on Tuesday as it secured an order from Scandinavian Airline SAS for up to 55 aircraft, in a deal worth ~$4B excluding purchase rights.

The deal will make Scandinavia's biggest carrier one of the largest operators of Embraer's (NYSE:ERJ) E2 family, and analysts said it signals positive demand for the planemaker's next-generation jetliners.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures up sharply as labor report comes above consensus
data.description:
Stock index futures rose sharply, while yields were higher on Thursday as nonfarm payrolls jumped more than expected in June.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.5%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 7 basis points to 4.36%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 10 basis points to 3.90%.

June nonfarm payrolls came in at +147K vs. +110K consensus and +144K in May (revised from +139K), while the unemployment rate was 4.1% vs. 4.3% consensus and +4.2% prior.

U.S. stocks ended mostly higher on Wednesday, as a positive trade development overshadowed an unexpected dip in U.S. June private sector employment.

On Wednesday, U.S. President Donald Trump said a trade deal had been reached with Vietnam, under which the U.S. will get “total access” to the Vietnamese market. Vietnam will pay a 20% tariff on goods sent into the U.S.

"It’s been an eventful 24 hours in markets, with a bunch of competing narratives across different asset classes. On the bright side, the S&P 500 hit a new record after the US reached a trade deal with Vietnam, which raised hopes they were about to announce multiple deals before the reciprocal tariff deadline on July 9," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

Other economic releases for the day include the initial jobless claims, which is expected to come in at 240K.

The June PMI Composite Final and the June ISM Service Index will land during market hours. The former is expected to come in at 52.8, and the latter at 50.8.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day.  

The stock market will close at 1 pm ET today on account of the Friday Independence Day holiday.

data.title: Needham drops its bearish view and moves to the sidelines on Meta Platforms
data.description:
Needham on Thursday took a more neutral stance on social media and tech giant Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) and gave it a "hold" from its previously assigned "underperform" rating.

Analyst Laura Martin thinks the rating reflects Meta's slowing labor productivity improvement, driven by rising headcount and higher costs per FTE, which she believes works against share price appreciation.

The research firm, however, expects the company to over-deliver on its prior revenue and margin estimates for Q2 and FY25 and forecasts that it will report 14% revenue growth and 6% EPS growth in 2025.

Needham chose not to turn bullish on Meta as the company's capital budgets continue to rise. It also sees a structural cost disadvantage against rivals like Google (GOOGL) (GOOG), Amazon (AMZN), and Microsoft (MSFT), who own their cloud assets, and regulatory risks related to privacy, antitrust, and content moderation, among other reasons.

"We continue to worry about META's rapidly rising CapX budgets, as it tries to catch up with companies much larger than itself in the LLM wars," the research firm said in its July 3 note. "We estimate that META's CapX will be $68B in FY25, up 84% y/y, the fastest growth among the hyperscalers. The ROIC on this CapX is uncertain, and the larger the spending, the more likely there is waste, in our view."

META does not have a price target from Needham at the moment. Stock is up about 22% so far this year, while the benchmark S&P index is up nearly 6%.

data.title: Initial jobless claims unexpectedly slip the previous week
data.description:
Initial jobless claims for the week ended June 28 stood at 230K, vs. 240K consensus and 237K the prior week, the Department of Labor said on Thursday.

The 4-week moving average was 241.50K, vs. the previous week's revised 245.25K.

data.title: US goods and services trade deficit expands in May
data.description:
• The U.S. international trade in goods deficit was $71.5 billion in May, up $11.3 billion from $60.3 billion in April, revised. 
  • May exports were $279.0 billion, $11.6 billion less than April exports.
  • May imports were $350.5 billion, $0.3 billion less than April imports.

data.title: Franklin Covey widens 2025 guidance to $265M–$275M amid cost reductions and market uncertainty
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Franklin Covey Co. (FC) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Paul Walker highlighted that "despite an external environment that continues to be uncertain and where, as a result of this uncertainty, organizations are scrutinizing costs at a greater level and delaying many investment decisions," third quarter revenue was $67.1 million, with adjusted EBITDA of $7.3 million, which Walker noted "was higher than the top end of our expected range of between $4 million and $6.5 million."
  • Walker stated the company completed a series of cost reductions designed "to offset the impact of these factors on our profitability," expecting these actions "will help offset declines in revenue related to government actions and to the general uncertainty in the economy."
  • The CEO reported strong traction from the go-to-market transformation in the Enterprise North America business, noting "we're really encouraged by the traction and the early results we're achieving" and described the restructuring of the sales force into dedicated teams for new and existing clients as complete.
  • Walker cited a notable new logo win with a $6 billion information management company, resulting in "a $2.3 million contract with about $1.7 million scheduled to deliver in the coming quarters."
  • The CEO also emphasized robust client retention and a "15-point increase in the number of clients who expanded outside of their renewal period compared to the third quarter last year."
  • Walker announced the appointment of Dariusz Paczuski as Chief Marketing Officer on June 1 to lead expanded marketing efforts.
  • In Education, Walker stated "we still expect to grow our Education business year-over-year on both the top and bottom lines and expect our addition of new schools and our school retention results to be as good, if not better this year than they were last year."
  • CFO Jessica Betjemann said, "Total revenue in the third quarter was $67.1 million and finished in line with our previously shared expectation, at the low end," and that "gross margin for the third quarter remained strong and was approximately flat year-over-year at 76.5% of revenue."
  • Betjemann explained, "We incurred $4.7 million in expenses for these restructuring activities, primarily for severance and related costs," and the cost actions "resulted in savings of $3 million in this quarter and results in $4 million savings in Q4 and an annualized run rate savings of $8 million in fiscal year 2026."

Outlook

  • The company revised its fiscal 2025 guidance, now expecting revenue in the range of $265 million to $275 million.
  • Adjusted EBITDA guidance widened to $28 million to $33 million, with CFO Betjemann noting, "the midpoint and top end of this range is consistent with our previous guidance due to the cost reduction actions we took in the third quarter that will produce savings to beneficially impact this year."
  • Management explained the revision reflects "continued uncertainty impacting our clients' decision-making" and "the timing risk for the delivery of services for some contracts we have closed in the last 90 days and expect to close this quarter that could slip into the first quarter of the next fiscal year."
  • Updated guidance for fiscal year 2026 will be provided at year-end, but management expects "a meaningful increase in adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow in fiscal year 2026."

Financial Results

  • Third quarter revenue was $67.1 million, down 9% from the prior year quarter, but up 13% sequentially.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $7.3 million, exceeding the top end of the prior guidance range.
  • Operating expenses rose to $53.5 million, primarily due to a $4 million increase in restructuring charges and $1.6 million in higher SG&A tied to the new go-to-market initiative.
  • Free cash flow through the third quarter totaled $10.6 million compared to $30.6 million for the same period last year, with year-to-date cash flow from operating activities at $19 million.
  • At quarter end, liquidity was over $95 million, with $33.7 million in cash and no drawdowns on the $62.5 million credit facility.
  • During the quarter, the company repurchased approximately 372,000 shares for $8.3 million, with $27.9 million remaining under the current buyback authorization.

Q&A

  • Alexander Paris, Barrington Research, asked about additional milestones in the Enterprise division and pipeline growth. Paul Walker explained the company is "continuing to see our pipelines grow," highlighted strong marketing activity, and noted a 60% services attach rate in the Enterprise division.
  • Paris also questioned expectations for new schools in Education. Walker responded, "we do expect that the way that business is pacing is that we'll be there or potentially even a little bit better" compared to the prior year.
  • Paris sought clarification on the $10 million guidance reduction by bucket; Walker indicated the primary delta relates to the timing of service delivery, especially in Education and Enterprise.
  • Jeff Martin, ROTH Capital, inquired about the environment for fiscal '26. Walker said, "we're certainly planning with that in mind, that tariffs and other actions...that uncertainty, hopefully, it will eventually go away. But...we're not planning that it will."
  • Nehal Chokshi, Northland Capital Markets, pressed on why subscription revenue invoiced was down 8% year-over-year despite strong new logo and expansion activity. Walker cited client downsizing, smaller expansion dollar amounts, and a large client converting to an intellectual property agreement as key factors.
  • Chokshi questioned the wide Q4 revenue range. Betjemann stated, "the delivery of services and the timing for decision-making...is tight. There is a possibility, though. So the top end of the range is reflecting that we were able to close and do everything in this quarter, but there's a realistic view that it may not happen and things could get pushed into next year."
  • David Storms, Stonegate, asked about Education materials revenue volatility. Walker explained materials revenue fluctuates based on the timing and size of district/statewide contracts.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a slightly negative to neutral tone, focusing on guidance reductions, revenue timing uncertainty, and reconciling strong client metrics with falling subscription revenue. One analyst said, "given those 3 positive things, that would imply that invoice subscription revenue should be up, not down 8% year-over-year, even with these headwinds being cited."
  • Management maintained a confident but cautious tone, emphasizing cost actions, strategic initiatives, and future growth. Walker repeatedly stressed confidence: "we continue to be confident in the actions that we're undertaking to accelerate future growth."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management maintained a cautious approach, but management highlighted more concrete cost actions and a wider guidance range this quarter.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company revised annual revenue guidance down again, now to $265 million–$275 million, compared to $275 million–$285 million last quarter.
  • Adjusted EBITDA guidance widened to $28 million–$33 million, but the midpoint/top end remains in line with previous guidance due to increased cost savings.
  • Strategic focus shifted to cost reductions and maintaining investment in growth, while the previous quarter stressed the go-to-market transformation's early traction and expansion plans.
  • Analyst questions this quarter centered more on reconciling lower revenue with positive client activity, as well as the timing of revenue recognition and service delivery.
  • Management's tone remained cautiously optimistic, but with increased emphasis on prudent cost management and operational discipline.

Risks and Concerns

  • The uncertain macroeconomic environment and client cost scrutiny continue to impact sales cycles and service delivery timing, with management noting, "the uncertainty in the environment pushed some of these wins late enough into our year to create a timing risk."
  • Ongoing government actions, tariffs, and the end of ESSER funds present risks to both Enterprise and Education revenue.
  • Management cited the possibility of further timing-related revenue slippage into next fiscal year.
  • Analysts raised concerns about the wide revenue guidance range and the disconnect between client activity and subscription revenue trends.

Final Takeaway

Franklin Covey completed significant cost reductions and maintained strategic investment in growth initiatives, resulting in a widened annual guidance range of $265 million–$275 million revenue and $28 million–$33 million adjusted EBITDA. Management remains confident in the business model, citing strong client retention, expanding multiyear contracts, and early traction from go-to-market transformations, while acknowledging the impact of macroeconomic uncertainty and timing risks on near-term results. Looking ahead, the leadership expects actions taken this year to support a meaningful increase in adjusted EBITDA and free cash flow in fiscal 2026.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: HSBC to sell UK life unit to Chesnara for $355M
data.description:
HSBC (NYSE:HSBC) agreed to sell its HSBC Life (UK) Limited unit to Chesnara Plc for £260M  (US$355M), Chesnara said in a filing on Thursday.

For HSBC (NYSE:HSBC), the deal continues a string of asset sales as the bank focuses more on Asia, amid a broader restructuring push to cut costs and boost profits. Last month, the bank agreed to sell its custody business in Germany. Last year, it agreed to sell its French life insurance business.

In contrast, Chesnara is consolidating life and pension books in the U.K. and the Netherlands, complemented by business written across the group's businesses in Sweden, the Netherlands, and the U.K., it said.

The deal is expected to generate lifetime cash generation of over £800M for Chesnara, through delivery of future profits and run off of capital requirements over time, the company said in the filing.

HSBC Holdings (HSBC) stock was unchanged in U.S. premarket trading on Thursday.
